entry.title: South China Morning Post: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 8 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-08 22:39PM.
entry.content:

  • Hong Kong has halted its Covid flight suspension mechanism. Here’s what the shift means for travellers and airlines
  • New chief of Hong Kong’s anti-graft agency says decade of experience fighting corruption in prisons will serve him well
  • Home buyers snap up Sino Land’s Villa Garda flats in Lohas Park, enticed by 16 per cent discounts
  • US-China trade war: Competing interests tug at Joe Biden as he mulls cutting tariffs on Chinese goods
  • Beijing grapples with Shinzo Abe’s mixed legacy on China-Japan relations after former prime minister is shot dead
  • South Korean firms fret over China supply-chain disruptions - with ‘chaos’ to continue
  • Xi Jinping allies lay out Chinese president’s global risk outlook in countdown to Communist Party congress
  • Chinese embassy slams India’s tech firm probes after Vivo, Xiaomi and Huawei investigations, fines
  • Nationalists attack Chinese journalist over emotional Shinzo Abe report
  • China’s former police vice-minister Sun Lijun pleads guilty to bribery, stocks fraud
  • Sound can numb pain in mice, paving way for alternative treatment, Chinese scientist says
  • ByteDance executive who co-founded Musical.ly app, later absorbed by TikTok, quits tech unicorn amid corporate restructuring
  • China’s coronavirus recovery at ‘critical point’, says Li Keqiang, singling out five regions to carry the economy
  • China-Britain relations expected to face further challenges no matter who replaces Boris Johnson as prime minister
  • China to implement from September strict new cross-border data transfer rules, complicating operations of international firms
  • China-backed AIIB picks Abu Dhabi for first overseas office, more could follow
  • Air China responds to viral video of loose wing screw on Boeing 737-NG flight
  • Shinzo Abe: a political titan who shaped Japan’s policies on China, defence, economy
  • China premier stresses data security after alleged leak affecting 1 billion Chinese residents triggers concerns
  • Beijing delivers double warning to US on Taiwan Strait tensions
  • Lack of global consensus bodes ill for health of world’s oceans
  • How Shinzo Abe’s killer thwarted Japan’s strict gun laws
  • Shinzo Abe shooting: who’s the killer and what weapon did he use?
  • Coronavirus: ‘New wave’ in Malaysia driven by Omicron BA.5, health minister says
  • Russia leaves G20 Bali meeting early, as ‘frenzied criticism’ over Ukraine clouds Indonesia’s multilateralism push
  • Philippines’ Marcos tests positive for Covid-19 two days after meeting with China’s Wang Yi
  • World leaders from China to Singapore and Australia stunned by death of Japan’s Shinzo Abe
  • Philippine Nobel laureate Ressa loses appeal against cyber libel conviction
  • Shinzo Abe shooting: UK finance minister Nadhim Zahawi deletes tweet saying Japan ex-PM is dead
  • Did South Korean police use ‘excessive force’ to subdue a Vietnamese man holding a knife?
  • Japan’s former prime minister Shinzo Abe dies after attack by gunman
  • As Philippines economy stutters, can Marcos keep promises on more jobs, cheaper food?
  • Ukraine war: Is Vladimir Putin using oil to ‘blackmail’ Japan for slapping sanctions on Russia?
  • Elon Musk says he’s terminating US$44 billion Twitter deal
  • Daughter of Angola ex-president Jose Eduardo dos Santos demands autopsy on foul play fears
  • Russia and other nuclear-armed parties must be held to account for violations of non-proliferation treaty
  • Joe Biden signs executive order on abortion protection, calls US Supreme Court ‘out of control’
  • Prince Harry wins first stage in libel suit against UK newspaper
  • Contest to replace UK’s Boris Johnson begins, ‘someone has to grip this moment’, says top candidate
  • Why US talk of ‘friend-shoring’ supply chains makes no economic sense
  • China Great Wall International’s bond sees record slump as investors worry about a Huarong-like crisis
  • Fred Ma, former Hong Kong minister and FWD director, appointed chairman of insurer backed by Richard Li
  • China Resources buys into HQ of struggling Hong Kong cosmetic retailer Bonjour Holdings via joint venture for US$114.7 million
  • Luxury brand Tom Ford hires Goldman Sachs to explore potential sale that could be valued in the billions of dollars
  • Why Asia’s gradual Covid-19 recovery means it has less to fear from rising inflation
  • How Hong Kong’s longest-running life insurer will help drive city’s recovery as it marks 125th anniversary
  • YouTube says its TikTok rival Shorts is helping musicians rapidly grow subscribers
  • Hong Kong stocks gain on hopes of strengthening recovery amid easing of travel restrictions and improved Chinese data
  • Elon Musk’s dispute with Twitter over bots puts deal at risk, but social media company intends to complete merger
  • Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission CEO Ashley Alder to leave post in January to join UK regulator as chairman
  • Joel Kim Booster’s recent forays into movie producing and Netflix specials – and why his job ‘isn’t to represent all of you’
  • Lake Como villa that once hosted Winston Churchill now a luxury hotel, and Kate Moss and Martha Stewart are fans
  • Squid Game, BTS fuel interest in Korea, but Korean studies courses at universities don’t benefit – they lack funding to attract more students, say experts
  • Fine dining or casual, French restaurants in Hong Kong are alive and well, as recent openings Auor and Terroirs by LQV show
  • Vitamin D from sunshine could help keep dementia at bay, a study shows, and fight other diseases
  • K-drama actor and Twenty-Five Twenty-One star Nam Joo-hyuk hit by more allegations of school bullying
  • Worn by celebrities like Zendaya and model Emily Ratajkowski, fashion designer Nensi Dojaka’s clothes explore interplay between sensuality and strength
  • Xiaomi 12s Ultra review: the best 2022 phone, its Leica camera makes Apple iPhone 13 Pro photos look flat, and battery life is unbeatable
  • Gourmet meals for under US$5: 2022 Michelin Guide Singapore adds 9 entries to its Bib Gourmand list of low-cost restaurants and food stalls
  • In HBO Go’s epic series Legacy, set in 1920s Shanghai, three stepsisters look to take over their wealthy family’s business
  • Meet Extraordinary Attorney Woo’s Park Eun-bin, who appeared in a Taeyeon MV and alongside Lee Byung-hun and Squid Game’s Lee Jung-jae as a child star aged just six
  • Meet Ricky Martin’s adorable teen twins, Matteo and Valentino: the 50-year-old singer is raising his kids with hubby Jwan Yosef, enjoying the beach, jets and Grammys red carpets
  • Celebs who got swindled: Shark Tank star Barbara Corcoran almost lost US$400,000, Anne Hathaway got scammed out of US$2.4 million by her ex-boyfriend, and Robert De Niro got conned investing in art
  • Meet Shivon Zilis, Elon Musk’s baby mama who had twins: the tech whizz met the world’s richest man and Tesla CEO through her AI work – and fangirls over him on Twitter
  • The fabulous life of Penelope Disick: as Kourtney Kardashian and Scott Disick’s daughter turns 10, it’s clear the jet-setting, red-haired Yeezy and Skims wearing tween is a chip off the old block
  • How many children does Elon Musk have now? His 9 (and counting) kids revealed, from new twins with Shivon Zilis, and baby X Æ A-Xii, to trans daughter Vivian Wilson ... meet his brood and their mum
  • Inside Boris Johnson’s controversial lavish life as Britain’s prime minister, from his US$2 million net worth to dropping thousands on take-out, home refurbishments and that NYE trip
  • Selling Sunset’s Christine Quinn listed this US$34 million Miami penthouse through her new firm – take a look inside the ultra-luxury, 43rd floor home that you can even buy with cryptocurrency
  • How Fendi got East-West fusion right at Paris Fashion Week: Kim Jones’ autumn/winter 2022-23 haute couture collection made style stops in Kyoto, Paris and Rome – tastefully
  • 6 best new Hong Kong restaurants with spectacular sea views, from ADD+ and Mosu in West Kowloon’s M+, to Salisterra and Radical Chic, which have new chefs
  • Mukesh Ambani’s million-dollar motorcade: Asia’s second richest man owns the most expensive car in India, along with over 70 luxury rides including Bentleys and Rolls-Royces in his ‘Jio Garage’
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • CNA Explains: Who is at risk of monkeypox and how worried should you be?
  • Commentary: Boris Johnson resigns, but what and who comes next?
  • Klang Valley commuters are addicted to their cars. What can be done to encourage use of public transport?
  • IN FOCUS: From life-giving source to murky brown water, is there hope for Nepal’s Bagmati river?
  • Commentary: Travelling alone sounds daunting but you should consider it anyway
  • Commentary: What could help Singapore embrace plant-based meat and lab-grown chicken?
  • Elon Musk looks to abandon US$44 billion Twitter deal
  • US, China top diplomats meet in new bid to lower the temperature
  • Spain, Germany start Euro 2022 in style
  • Germany thrash Denmark to get Euro campaign off to a flying start
  • Florida man charged with selling fake Cisco equipment in $1 billion scheme
  • Aerojet Rocketdyne to pay $9 million to resolve allegations of US cybersecurity violations
  • US FDA grants full approval to Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine for ages 12-15
  • Ukraine pleads for more weapons as defiant Russia warns West
  • Police arrest fan after racism allegations at England v India test
  • Djokovic happy, not surprised, Kyrgios is in final
  • Estonian PM reaches agreement to regain majority in parliament
  • Di Maria completes move to Juventus
  • Wimbledon semis run leaves Britain's Norrie yearning for more
  • Di Maria completes Juventus move

  • Biden to meet with advisers on China tariffs, decision timing unclear
  • Shinzo Abe assassination raises questions about Japan's VIP security
  • Assassination suspect says he held grudge against 'organisation' Shinzo Abe was connected to: Japan police

  • Russia to improve crypto transaction monitoring as regulation draws closer

  • UK think tank calls for global digital currency rules

  • Yellen heads to Asia to build support for price cap on Russian oil
  • Cyber insurance firm Coalition valued at $5 billion after fund raise
  • US mulls fresh bid to restrict chipmaking tools for China's SMIC: Sources
  • China publishes action plan to improve domestic and global standard-setting
  • First shipment of infant formula ingredient from Singapore plant leaves for US
  • Vivian Balakrishnan urges G20 countries to stop 'weaponising food'
  • Blood donations needed in Singapore as stocks of A+ and O+ blood run low
  • Singapore confirms fourth case of monkeypox infection since June
  • Singapore orders recall of two Haagen-Dazs ice cream products due to presence of pesticide

  • Tan Boon Hui, Arts House Limited executive director and former Singapore Art Museum director, dies at 53

  • Worker dies in forklift accident at Keat Hong Link; workplace fatalities rise to 29 this year

  • Spain come from behind to beat Finland 4-1 in Women's Euros
  • Jesus leads Arsenal comeback in 5-3 win
  • Verstappen takes Austrian sprint pole, misery for Mercedes
  • Spain's Reina returns to Villarreal for second spell
  • Hamilton apologises to Mercedes after qualifying crash
  • Another year, another new champion as Jabeur and Rybakina face off

  • From fierce critic to 'weird' bromance, Kyrgios opens up about Djokovic
  • England's Smith lands two new cars with one brilliant swing
  • Pogacar pips Vingegaard to extend Tour lead with stage seven win

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Shinzo Abe dies after being shot in Nara with homemade gun

  • G-20 diplomats face unity headwinds on Ukraine, war’s impact

  • Rival toymakers join forces to release best of Gundam, Zoids

  • EDITORIAL: Parties should offer clear ideas to pay for social security burden
  • VOX POPULI: Tokyo changing ‘child care leave’ to ‘work’ reflects power of words
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 8 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Boris Johnson Quits Amid Latest Scandal, a Messy End to a Messy Tenure
  • Johnson’s Lies Worked for Years, Until They Didn’t
  • Quitting Oil Income Is Hard, Even for States That Want Climate Action
  • Brittney Griner Pleads Guilty to Drug Charges in Russian Court
  • Democrats Propose Raising Taxes on Some High Earners to Bolster Medicare
  • Jobs Aplenty, but a Shortage of Care Keeps Many Women From Benefiting
  • Russian artillery falls on Kramatorsk, an already devastated city now in the line of assault.
  • To Pick Johnson’s Successor, Britain’s Conservatives Confront the Void He Leaves Behind
  • Brittney Griner’s Supporters Hold Steady After Guilty Plea
  • Raiders Make N.F.L. History With Team President Hire
  • Rafael Nadal Withdraws From Wimbledon Ahead of Semifinal Match
  • No. 2 Theranos Executive Found Guilty of 12 Counts of Fraud
  • How Wish Built (and Fumbled) a Dollar Store for the Internet
  • Glacier Tragedy Shows Reach of Europe’s New Heat
  • How Unlikely Is It That the Audits of Comey and McCabe Were a Coincidence? A Statistical Exploration.
  • Monkeypox Vaccine Rollout Is Marred by Glitches in New York
  • James Caan, Actor Who Won Fame in ‘The Godfather,’ Dies at 82
  • Robert Colescott Throws Down the Gauntlet
  • ‘Thor: Love and Thunder’ Review: A God’s Comic Twilight
  • Chinese Police Database Was Left Unsecured Long Before Hackers Seized It
  • More Fed officials back a big July rate move, but downplay recession fears.
  • Behind the fight between Ben & Jerry’s and its owner, Unilever.
  • An Irish regulator puts Facebook data policies back in the spotlight.
  • Shell sees refining profits nearly tripling, adding $1 billion to its bottom line.
  • Amazon Hub in Newark Is Canceled After Unions and Local Groups Object
  • Match Group suspends some political donations after abortion ruling.
  • Buffalo’s ‘Other Story’ Is Told in Redevelopment and Growth
  • Boris Johnson Made a Terrible Mistake: He Apologized
  • Why Mass Shooters Do the Evil They Do
  • That Was the Stagflation That Was
  • Americans Are Losing Their Right to Not Conform
  • What’s Wrong With Health Insurance? Deductibles Are Ridiculous, for Starters.
  • An American ‘Dystopia,’ After Roe
  • A Whale Feeding Frenzy in Antarctica Signals a Conservation Success
  • Rotterdam Won’t Dismantle Bridge to Allow Jeff Bezos’ Superyacht Through
  • For Foreign Fighters in Ukraine, a War Unlike Any They’ve Seen
  • Here are the contenders to become the next prime minister.
  • For Boris Johnson, a Tumultuous Tenure Ends With a Messy Exit
  • Beijing Introduces a Local Vaccine Pass and Urges Older People to Get Shots
  • U.S. pharmacists receive permission from the F.D.A. to prescribe Pfizer’s Covid pills.
  • The pandemic has eroded Americans’ trust in experts and elected leaders alike, a survey finds.
  • After Roe, South Carolina Considers More Restrictions on Abortion
  • Texas Governor Orders State Police to Return Migrants to Border
  • Killer of George Floyd Sentenced to 21 Years for Violating Civil Rights

  • I.R.S. Asks Inspector General to Review Audits of Comey and McCabe

  • Witnesses in Young Thug Case Have Been Threatened, Prosecutors Say
  • Jerry Harris Sentenced to 12 Years for Sex Crimes Involving Minors
  • Secret Service Director to Step Down and Join Maker of Snapchat
  • Alaska Kills 4 Bears That Entered Homeless Camp
